root_text.an8.25:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sakkesu viharati kapilavatthusmiṁ nigrodhārāme.
root_text.an8.25:1.2: Atha kho mahānāmo sakko yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho mahānāmo sakko bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an8.25:1.4: “Yato kho, mahānāma, buddhaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, dhammaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti, saṅghaṁ saraṇaṁ gato hoti;
root_text.an8.25:2.2: “Yato kho, mahānāma, upāsako pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti, adinnādānā paṭivirato hoti, kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti, musāvādā paṭivirato hoti, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato hoti;
root_text.an8.25:2.3: ettāvatā kho, mahānāma, upāsako sīlavā hotī”ti.
root_text.an8.25:3.1: “Kittāvatā pana, bhante, upāsako attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāyā”ti?
root_text.an8.25:3.2: “Yato kho, mahānāma, upāsako attanāva saddhāsampanno hoti, no paraṁ saddhāsampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:3.3: attanāva sīlasampanno hoti, no paraṁ sīlasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:3.4: attanāva cāgasampanno hoti, no paraṁ cāgasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:3.5: attanāva bhikkhūnaṁ dassanakāmo hoti, no paraṁ bhikkhūnaṁ dassane samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:3.6: attanāva saddhammaṁ sotukāmo hoti, no paraṁ saddhammassavane samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:3.7: attanāva sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhāraṇajātiko hoti, no paraṁ dhammadhāraṇāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:3.8: attanāva sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti, no paraṁ atthūpaparikkhāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:3.9: attanāva atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti, no paraṁ dhammānudhammappaṭipattiyā samādapeti.
root_text.an8.25:3.10: Ettāvatā kho, mahānāma, upāsako attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāyā”ti.
root_text.an8.25:4.1: “Kittāvatā pana, bhante, upāsako attahitāya ca paṭipanno hoti parahitāya cā”ti?
root_text.an8.25:4.2: “Yato kho, mahānāma, upāsako attanā ca saddhāsampanno hoti, parañca saddhāsampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:4.3: attanā ca sīlasampanno hoti, parañca sīlasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:4.4: attanā ca cāgasampanno hoti, parañca cāgasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:4.5: attanā ca bhikkhūnaṁ dassanakāmo hoti, parañca bhikkhūnaṁ dassane samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:4.6: attanā ca saddhammaṁ sotukāmo hoti, parañca saddhammassavane samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:4.7: attanā ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ dhāraṇajātiko hoti, parañca dhammadhāraṇāya samādapeti;
root_text.an8.25:4.8: attanā ca sutānaṁ dhammānaṁ atthūpaparikkhitā hoti, parañca atthūpaparikkhāya samādapeti,
root_text.an8.25:4.9: attanā ca atthamaññāya dhammamaññāya dhammānudhammappaṭipanno hoti, parañca dhammānudhammappaṭipattiyā samādapeti.
root_text.an8.25:4.10: Ettāvatā kho, mahānāma, upāsako attahitāya ca paṭipanno hoti parahitāya cā”ti.

translation_text.an8.25:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Sakyans, near Kapilavatthu in the Banyan Tree Monastery.
translation_text.an8.25:1.2: Then Mahānāma the Sakyan went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.an8.25:1.3: “Sir, how is a lay follower defined?”
translation_text.an8.25:1.4: “Mahānāma, when you’ve gone for refuge to the Buddha, the teaching, and the Saṅgha,
translation_text.an8.25:1.5: you’re considered to be a lay follower.”
translation_text.an8.25:2.1: “But how is an ethical lay follower defined?”
translation_text.an8.25:2.2: “When a lay follower doesn’t kill living creatures, steal, commit sexual misconduct, lie, or consume beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants,
translation_text.an8.25:2.3: they’re considered to be an ethical lay follower.”
translation_text.an8.25:3.1: “But how do we define a lay follower who is practicing to benefit themselves, not others?”
translation_text.an8.25:3.2: “A lay follower is accomplished in faith, but doesn’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:3.3: They’re accomplished in ethical conduct, but don’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:3.4: They’re accomplished in generosity, but don’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:3.5: They like to see the mendicants, but don’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:3.6: They like to hear the true teaching, but don’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:3.7: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard, but don’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:3.8: They examine the meaning of the teachings they’ve memorized, but don’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:3.9: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching, but they don’t encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:3.10: That’s how we define a lay follower who is practicing to benefit themselves, not others.”
translation_text.an8.25:4.1: “But how do we define a lay follower who is practicing to benefit both themselves and others?”
translation_text.an8.25:4.2: “A lay follower is accomplished in faith and encourages others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:4.3: They’re accomplished in ethical conduct and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:4.4: They’re accomplished in generosity and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:4.5: They like to see the mendicants and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:4.6: They like to hear the true teaching and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:4.7: They readily memorize the teachings they’ve heard and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:4.8: They examine the meaning of the teachings they’ve memorized and encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:4.9: Understanding the meaning and the teaching, they practice in line with the teaching and they encourage others to do the same.
translation_text.an8.25:4.10: That’s how we define a lay follower who is practicing to benefit both themselves and others.”
